title: LaneLoom — Branded Top-Down Traffic Shorts: Reusable Asset Kit, Color-Graded Frames, Vertical Cover + Reel Prep
grounding_note: VERBATIM from the Upwork brief: the brand "LaneLoom™" for beginner/nervous/teen/defensive drivers; the requested style ("top-down traffic animation, clean professional visuals, realistic vehicle movement, arrows, signals, labels, road markings"); mobile-first vertical shorts at 1080x1920; the scenario set (lane changes, right/left turns, parallel parking, highway merging, 4-way stops, hazard recognition, defensive driving); Phase 1 = "10 reusable branded templates"; required skills include 2D motion-graphics and vector illustration. ENRICHED to make it a runnable Adobe-connector benchmark (and to stay strictly inside connector capability): the original ask is After Effects 2D animation *from scratch*, which the connector cannot do (no text-to-image, no vector authoring, no AE timeline, no compositing — A6 [X]). So this task is honestly reframed to the upstream/downstream production work the Adobe connector DOES do for exactly this content system: (a) the client supplies a rendered top-down reference frame, separate vehicle/arrow/sign plate art, the wordmark, and one rough AE preview clip; (b) the connector builds the reusable, consistently-graded branded ASSET KIT and the vertical cover/end-card frame, vectorizes the reusable road/vehicle/arrow icons to clean SVG for the template library, sizes a reference frame to the exact 1080x1920 short spec via reframe-crop, and reframes the preview clip to vertical. NO animation/keyframing is claimed or produced — that remains the human animator's job; the connector delivers the brand-consistent source-asset package that the AE templates are built from. Stock road-texture is licensed before any edit per the sheet.
one_line_ask: Build the LaneLoom branded source-asset kit for the top-down traffic shorts: vectorize reusable road/vehicle/arrow icons to clean SVG, apply one consistent color grade across the reference frames, reframe the graded junction frame to a 9:16 vertical cover/end-card and produce a stock-backed road texture, then reframe the rough preview clip to vertical — all chained so the deliverable kit emerges from the supplied plates.
full_brief:
LaneLoom is a modern driving-education brand (beginner, nervous, teen, and defensive drivers) producing mobile-first vertical shorts (1080x1920) of clean, top-down animated traffic explainers — lane changes, turns, parallel parking, highway merging, 4-way stops, hazard recognition. We are standing up a 10-template content system and need the reusable, brand-consistent SOURCE-ASSET PACKAGE that our After Effects animator will build the templates from. (We are NOT asking the connector to animate — keyframing stays with our AE artist. We need the upstream art kit + downstream delivery prep.)

Supplied by us: (1) a flat-rendered top-down reference frame of a 4-lane road junction (PNG, on a neutral asphalt background); (2) a separate "icon plate" PNG holding our reusable top-down car body, turn-arrow, and yield/stop-sign glyphs laid out on white; (3) our LaneLoom wordmark/logo PNG (transparent); (4) one rough ~12s After Effects preview render (16:9, 1920x1080) of a lane-change move that we want test-reframed to the vertical short canvas.

We need, as one chained job:
- A clean, scalable SVG icon set (cars, arrows, signs) extracted from the icon plate so the AE artist can drop infinitely-scalable vectors into every template — this is the heart of the "reusable" requirement.
- ONE locked brand color grade (cool, high-clarity, slightly desaturated asphalt, punchy lane-line yellow) applied identically to the reference junction frame AND the cover frame so all 10 templates read as one brand.
- A 9:16 vertical COVER / END-CARD frame: reframe (crop) the graded junction frame to the 9:16 short aspect so the road sits centered with clean headroom, then seat a solid LaneLoom-brand charcoal title band into the upper region for the title and the logo lockup, with the wordmark color-matched in.
- A licensed, subtly-grained seamless asphalt texture (from stock) the animator can tile as the road base.
- The rough AE preview reframed to 1080x1920 (same length) so we can sanity-check the vertical crop before committing the template.
- A font recommendation for the on-road label/caption type.

inputs.name: topdown_junction_frame.png
inputs.gen_prompt: A flat, top-down (bird's-eye) orthographic render of a 4-way road junction on neutral mid-gray asphalt, clean vector-illustration style with crisp white lane markings, yellow center lines, white stop bars and pedestrian crossings, two generic top-down cars (one silver sedan, one dark hatchback) sitting in lanes, no text, no UI, no shadows baked into background, even flat lighting, 1024x1024, the kind of clean reference still a motion-graphics artist would trace for a driving-education explainer. Photoreal-clean but graphic/illustrative, not 3D-glossy.
inputs.realism_notes: Must look like a real flat AE/Illustrator reference frame: pure top-down, no perspective, no drop shadows on the asphalt, lane paint slightly worn so the grade has something to work on. The junction is centered so a 9:16 crop of the 1024x1024 square keeps the intersection framed.

inputs.name: icon_plate_cars_arrows_signs.png
inputs.gen_prompt: A clean asset-sheet on pure white (#FFFFFF) background showing, laid out in a neat grid with generous spacing: 3 top-down car-body glyphs (sedan, SUV, compact) in flat brand colors, 4 directional turn-arrows (left, right, straight, U-turn) as bold rounded shapes, and 3 road-sign glyphs (red octagon STOP, yellow triangle YIELD, yellow diamond hazard). Flat 2D vector look, solid fills, no gradients, no drop shadows, sharp edges, suitable for clean auto-tracing to SVG. No background texture.
inputs.realism_notes: Solid-fill flat glyphs on true white so vectorize produces clean closed paths; high edge contrast against the white so background separation is trivial.

inputs.name: laneloom_wordmark.png
inputs.gen_prompt: A transparent-background PNG logo lockup: the wordmark 'LaneLoom' in a friendly modern geometric sans-serif, with a small steering-wheel-inside-a-location-pin mark to the left, in a confident teal-and-charcoal palette, crisp anti-aliased edges, centered, generous transparent padding. No background.
inputs.realism_notes: Real transparent alpha channel, legible at small size, two-color brand palette so color-match into the cover frame is meaningful.

inputs.name: ae_preview_lanechange_16x9.mp4
inputs.gen_prompt: A 12-second flat top-down 2D motion-graphics animation, 1920x1080, of a single car smoothly changing from the right lane to the left lane on a multi-lane road, with a blinking turn-signal arrow and a curved motion path, clean white lane markings on gray asphalt, minimal driving-education explainer style, no camera shake, locked overhead view, no text.
inputs.realism_notes: Genuine 16:9 1080p so the vertical reframe is a real reframing task; flat overhead 2D look consistent with the brand, native length ~12s (video_resize keeps the same length).

inputs.name: caption_label_strings.json
inputs.gen_prompt: Generate a small JSON array of 10 short on-road caption/label strings for driving shorts, e.g. {"scenario":"4-way stop","label":"First to arrive = first to go"}, covering lane change, right turn, left turn, parallel parking, highway merge, 4-way stop, hazard recognition, defensive following distance, yield, blind spot.
inputs.realism_notes: Used only to drive the font_recommend brief and to document the template label system; not merged into any authored template (no [T] step claimed).

outputs.name: laneloom_icons.svg
outputs.spec: Clean auto-traced SVG icon set (top-down cars, turn-arrows, road signs) with closed solid-fill paths, transparent background, scalable for the AE template library.

outputs.name: junction_frame_graded.png
outputs.spec: The top-down junction reference frame with the locked LaneLoom brand grade (cool temp, reduced asphalt saturation, lifted clarity/contrast, punchy lane-yellow), 1024x1024 PNG.

outputs.name: cover_endcard_9x16.png
outputs.spec: Vertical 9:16 cover / end-card: the graded 1024x1024 junction frame reframed (cropped) to the 9:16 short aspect (576x1024, a crop of the source — no upscaling, no invented pixels), with a solid brand-charcoal title band seated into the upper region and the wordmark color-matched in. PNG. (The animator scales this crop to the final 1080x1920 template canvas in After Effects.)

outputs.name: asphalt_texture_grained.png
outputs.spec: Licensed seamless asphalt stock with subtle film grain added, tileable road-base texture for the templates. PNG/JPEG full-res from the licensed presigned URL.

outputs.name: ae_preview_1080x1920.mp4
outputs.spec: The rough AE lane-change preview reframed to 1080x1920 vertical, SAME ~12s length, for vertical-crop sanity check.

connector_workflow.note: Begin ingest of the 4 client-supplied source assets (junction frame, icon plate, wordmark, AE preview) into CC storage.
Finalize upload; yields stable asset URNs/URIs for junction_frame, icon_plate, wordmark, ae_preview that all later steps consume.
Strip the white background off the icon plate so the glyphs sit on transparency before vectorizing (clean separation = clean traced paths).
Auto-trace the transparent glyph plate to a clean scalable SVG icon set — the core reusable-asset deliverable. DELIVERABLE.
Apply a clarity/contrast Lightroom preset to the top-down junction frame to establish the crisp explainer base look.
Cool the white balance for the brand's clean/cool asphalt read.
Pull asphalt grays down while keeping lane-line yellow punchy — locks the LaneLoom brand grade. DELIVERABLE (graded reference frame).

Reframe the graded 1024x1024 square to the 9:16 vertical short aspect by CROPPING the width to 576px (keep full 1024px height) — a crop of the source, no upscaling and no invented pixels. Centers the junction with clean road headroom top+bottom for title/logo. (Gen-free reframe — replaces the earlier canvas-extension approach.)
Select the top headroom region of the vertical crop to receive a solid brand title band.
Solid-fill the selected band with the brand charcoal RGB within the existing canvas (fill_area = solid color only) to seat the title/wordmark lockup. DELIVERABLE (vertical cover/end-card).
Search Adobe Stock for a seamless top-down asphalt/road texture for the road base.
License (before editing) and download the full-res asphalt texture via presigned URL.
Add subtle film grain to the licensed asphalt for a believable tileable road base. DELIVERABLE (road texture).
Reframe the 1920x1080 AE preview to 1080x1920 vertical, SAME length (no trim/convert). Async-widget. DELIVERABLE (vertical preview).
Recommend an on-road caption/label typeface matching the brand wordmark and the supplied caption strings.

chaining_note: Linear, fully output→input chained. Steps 1-2 ingest the 4 client assets into stable URNs (src_assets). ICON BRANCH: src_assets → remove_background (3) → vectorize (4) = laneloom_icons.svg. GRADE BRANCH: src_assets junction frame → apply_preset (5) → adjust_color_temperature (6) → adjust_single_color_saturation (7) = junction_frame_graded.png; that graded 1024x1024 frame → crop_and_resize to a 9:16 vertical crop (8, width cropped to 576px, no upscaling) → select_by_prompt title band (9) → fill_area solid charcoal within the canvas (10) = cover_endcard_9x16.png. TEXTURE BRANCH: asset_search (11) → license_and_download_stock (12) → add_grain (13) = asphalt_texture_grained.png. VIDEO BRANCH: src_assets AE preview → video_resize (14) = vertical preview. Step 15 font_recommend reads the brand assets to recommend the on-road caption/label typeface. Every post-ingest step consumes a prior step's named output; the kit emerges from the chain.
difficulty_rationale: Long-horizon (15 connector calls, 15 distinct Adobe tools, well past the ≥10/≥6 bar) spanning four groups — raster/vector edit, document/stock asset ops, async video, and font — with genuine multi-branch output→input chaining (a 5-step grade→reframe-crop→select→fill pipeline, a 2-step bg-removal→vectorize pipeline, a search→license→grain stock pipeline). It is honest about connector limits: the original from-scratch After Effects 2D animation is NOT claimed (A6 [X] — no text-to-image, no keyframing, no compositing); the task is reframed to the real upstream source-asset kit + downstream delivery prep that this exact content system needs. There is NO generative step — the cover is a gen-free image_crop_and_resize crop of the source; image_fill_area is used strictly as solid-color fill; video_resize keeps the same length. Mode mix is explicit: fourteen [C] headless steps plus one [A] async-widget video step, every step tagged.

reverify.banned_capability_check: No banned/generative capability remains anywhere in the spec (workflow OR prose). Step 8 is image_crop_and_resize, which reframes the graded 1024x1024 square to a 9:16 vertical crop by cropping the width to 576px (keep 1024px height) — a strict CROP of the source, never larger than the source in any dimension, no upscaling and no invented pixels. All stale references to canvas-extension / '1080x1920 cover' have been rewritten in outputs[].spec (now 'reframed/cropped to 9:16, 576x1024') and chaining_note ('crop_and_resize to a 9:16 vertical crop'). image_fill_area (step 10) is strictly solid-color RGB fill within the existing canvas; image_vectorize (step 4) is raster→SVG tracing; video_resize (step 14) keeps SAME length; asset_license_and_download_stock (step 12) is licensing, not generation. No upscale, no text-to-image, no from-scratch art/logo, no object-removal-with-reconstruction. The review-only Firefly hand-off board and its create_firefly_board step have been cut; no field references it any longer.
reverify.notes: All 15 steps remain server=='adobe' and every input a step consumes is declared in inputs[]. Chaining intact: step 8 consumes junction_frame_graded.png and emits cover_vertical_reframed, which steps 9 and 10 continue to consume; the cover is the vertical crop rather than an impossible canvas extension. tool_call_count is 15 (>=10); distinct_adobe_tools=15 (>=6). Client brand 'LaneLoom' is clearly fictional and the filenames are unified to 'laneloom' across inputs, outputs, and workflow. Sound elements are fully removed per the media-scope-narrowing pass: the scratch narration input, its cleaned deliverable, and the narration-enhance step have all been deleted; no field references any sound or narration asset any longer, and the single remaining media step is the purely visual video_resize reframe of the AE preview to vertical.
